# easyRasch: Rasch analysis in R

R package for Rasch Measurement Theory psychometrics, intended for use
with [Quarto](https://quarto.org) for documentation and presentation of
analysis process and results.

This package largely functions as a wrapper for other R packages for the
Rasch analyses, primarily
[eRm](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/eRm/),
[mirt](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/mirt/),
[psychotree](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/psychotree/),
[iarm](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/iarm/),
[catR](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/catR/index.html), and
[TAM](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/TAM/index.html).

If you use `easyRasch` for a publication, please also cite these
packages. You can find citation information in R by using
`citation('packagename')`.

The package is intended to simplify the Rasch analysis process and
provides easy creation of tables and figures with functions that have
few options. The package has been tested on MacOS and Windows with R 4.1
to 4.4.

I have also created a package for Bayesian Rasch models, based on the
[`brms`](https://paulbuerkner.com/brms/) package. You can find
[`easyRaschBayes`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRaschBayes/index.html) on
[CRAN](https://cloud.r-project.org/web/packages/easyRaschBayes/index.html).

**NOTE: this package was formerly known as `RISEkbmRasch`. The old
GitHub page with code and commit history for `RISEkbmRasch` will remain
available as a public archive in read-only state.**

Please regularly check the
[Changelog](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/news/index.html) for notes
on updates.

There is a
[vignette](https://pgmj.github.io/raschrvignette/RaschRvign.html) that
is recommended reading after you skimmed this README. You will find a
sample Rasch analysis in the vignette, with output from most of the
package functions. The vignette is produced using Quarto, and its source
code is of course also
[available](https://github.com/pgmj/pgmj.github.io/blob/main/raschrvignette/RaschRvign.qmd).

Most functions have been developed for analysis of polytomous data (more
than two response categories), using the Rasch partial credit model
(PCM). Also, the choice was made to rely primarily on conditional
maximum likelihood (CML) estimation for item parameters, since it is
robust under various conditions and enables “person-free assessment”.

## Installation

**MacOS users NOTE: If you have MacOS Tahoe 26.1 or later, you may need
to add the code below to your R script BEFORE loading the `iarm`
package**

``` r
options(rgl.useNULL = TRUE)
```

First, install the [`pak`](https://pak.r-lib.org/) package:

``` r
install.packages('pak')
```

(If you have the `devtools` package installed, you can instead use
`devtools::install_github("pgmj/easyRasch")`)

Then install the package and its dependencies:

``` r
pak::pkg_install("pgmj/easyRasch")
```

While not strictly necessary, it is highly recommended to install Quarto
(and update your Rstudio and R installation if needed):

- <https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/>
- <https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/>

### Upgrading

``` r
detach("package:easyRasch", unload = TRUE) # not needed if you haven't loaded the package in your current session
pak::pkg_install("pgmj/easyRasch")
```

## Using the package

Load the package:

``` r
library(easyRasch)
# if you have a modern Mac, running MacOS 26.1 or later, you need this next line
options(rgl.useNULL = TRUE) # if you don't use MacOS, this line is not necessary
library(iarm)
```

Most functions in this package are relatively simple wrappers that
create outputs such as tables and figures to make the Rasch analysis
process quick and visual. The primary introduction to using the package
is the
[vignette](https://pgmj.github.io/raschrvignette/RaschRvign.html).

There are two data structure requirements:

1.  you need to create a dataframe object named `itemlabels` that
    consists of two variables/columns:

- the **first variable** named `itemnr`, containing variable names
  exactly as they are named in the dataframe containing data (for
  example q1, q2, q3, etc)
- the **second variable** named `item`, containing either the
  questionnaire item or a description of it (or description of a task,
  etc)

Sample code for four items:

``` r
itemlabels <- data.frame(itemnr = paste0("q",1:4),
                         item = c("Was the package easy to install?", 
                                  "Did you run in to any issues?", 
                                  "Have you read the 'known issues' section?", 
                                  "Does this guide help?")
                         )
```

2.  the response data you want to analyze needs to be in a dataframe
    with participants as rows and items as columns/variables, with ONLY
    response data in the dataframe.

- **the lowest response category needs to be zero (0)** for all items.
  See
  <https://pgmj.github.io/datawrangling.html#recoding-response-categories>
  for sample R code for recoding.
- you will need to separate any demographic variables into a separate
  dataframe or separate vectors (preferrably as labeled factors), for
  analysis of differential item functioning (DIF). Then remove your
  DIF-variables from the dataframe with item data. **The dataframe with
  item data can only contain item data for the analysis functions to
  work** (no ID variable or other demographic variables).

For most Rasch functions, there is auto-detection of dichotomous or
polytomous data, and For some Rasch-analysis functions in the package,
there are separate functions for polytomous data (more than two response
options for each item) and dichotomous/binary data. The Rating Scale
Model (RSM) for polytomous data has not been implemented in any of the
functions.

These functions use PCM as default and you can use the option
`model = "RM"` for dichotomous data:

- [`RItargeting()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RItargeting.md)
- [`RItif()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RItif.md)
- [`RIloadLoc()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIloadLoc.md)
- [`RIpfit()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIpfit.md)

### Notes on known issues

There are currently few or no checks on whether data input in functions
are correct. This means that you need to make sure to follow the
instructions above, or you may have unexpected outputs or difficult to
interpret error messages. Start by using the functions for descriptive
analysis and look closely at the output, which usually reveals mistakes
in data coding or demographic variables left in the item dataset.

If there is too much missingness in your data, some functions may have
issues or take a lot of time to run. In the Quarto template file there
is a script for choosing how many responses a participant needs to have
to be included in the analysis. You can experiment with this if you run
in to trouble.

Currently, the
[`RIloadLoc()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIloadLoc.md)
function does not work with any missing data (due to the underlying PCA
function), and the workaround for now is to run this command with
[`na.omit()`](https://rdrr.io/r/stats/na.fail.html) for the dataframe
(ie. `RIloadLoc(na.omit(df))`. Other reasons for functions taking longer
time to run is having a lot of items (30+), and/or if you have a lot of
response categories that are disordered (often happens with more than
4-6 response categories, especially if they are unlabeled in the
questionnaire).

### For the curious

For those new to R, it may be useful to know that you can easily access
the code in each function by using the base R
[`View()`](https://rdrr.io/r/utils/View.html) function. For example,
`View(RItargeting)` shows the code for the
[`RItargeting()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RItargeting.md)
function that creates a Wright map style figure (after installing and
loading the easyRasch package). You can also find the documentation/help
on each command by using the command
[`?RItargeting`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RItargeting.md)
in the console (replace `RItargeting` with the function you are
interested in).

If you are new to R, [Hadley Wickham’s book “R for data
science”](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/) is a great place to start. Also have
a look at [Introduction to R with
Tidyverse](https://introduction-r-tidyverse.netlify.app/) by Sophie Lee.

## Author

[Magnus Johansson](https://ki.se/en/people/magnus-johansson-3) is a
licensed psychologist with a PhD in behavior analysis. He works as a
research specialist at [Karolinska
Institutet](https://ki.se/en/cns/research/centre-for-psychiatry-research),
Department of Clinical Neuroscience, Center for Psychiatry Research.

- ORCID: [0000-0003-1669-592X](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1669-592X)
- Bluesky:
  [@pgmj.bsky.social](https://bsky.app/profile/pgmj.bsky.social)
- Mastodon: [@pgmj@scicomm.xyz](https://scicomm.xyz/@pgmj)

# Package index

## All functions

- [`RI_iarm_ICCplot()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RI_iarm_ICCplot.md)
  : Item Characteristic Curves

- [`RI_iarm_person_estimates()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RI_iarm_person_estimates.md)
  :

  Temporary fix for upstream bug in
  [`iarm::person_estimates()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/iarm/man/person_estimates.html)

- [`RI_iarm_persons_mle()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RI_iarm_persons_mle.md)
  :

  Temporary fix for upstream bug in
  [`iarm::person_estimates()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/iarm/man/person_estimates.html)

- [`RIallresp()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIallresp.md)
  : Create table with summarized responses across all items.

- [`RIbardiv()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIbardiv.md)
  : Create a stacked diverging bar graph to show response distribution

- [`RIbarplot()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIbarplot.md)
  : Create individual bar plots for all items.

- [`RIbarstack()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIbarstack.md)
  : Create a stacked bar graph to show response distribution

- [`RIbootLRT()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIbootLRT.md)
  : Bootstrapped Likelihood Ratio Test

- [`RIbootPCA()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIbootPCA.md)
  : Parametric bootstrap procedure for PCA of Rasch model residuals

- [`RIbootRestscore()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIbootRestscore.md)
  : Item-restscore bootstrapped

- [`RIcheckdata()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIcheckdata.md)
  : Check dataframe

- [`RIciccPlot()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIciccPlot.md)
  : Conditional Item Characteristic Curves

- [`RIcolorlistitems()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIcolorlistitems.md)
  : Show items based on itemlabels file, with coloring options

- [`RIcrel()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIcrel.md) :
  Conditional reliability using RMU

- [`RIdemographics()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdemographics.md)
  : Create table for demographic variables

- [`RIdifFigThresh()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifFigThresh.md)
  : Create a DIF line graph for item PCM thresholds

- [`RIdifFigTime()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifFigTime.md)
  : Create a DIF line graph over time, showing groups' PCM item
  locations

- [`RIdifFigure()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifFigure.md)
  : Create a DIF line graph, showing groups' PCM item locations

- [`RIdifFigureLR()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifFigureLR.md)
  : DIF PCM analysis with panel figure output for items' average
  locations

- [`RIdifFigureRM()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifFigureRM.md)
  : Create a DIF line graph, showing groups' RM item locations

- [`RIdifTable()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifTable.md)
  : DIF analysis - requires having set up dif.variables previously

- [`RIdifTable2()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifTable2.md)
  : DIF PCM interaction analysis

- [`RIdifTableLR()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifTableLR.md)
  : DIF PCM analysis with table output for item locations

- [`RIdifTableRM()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifTableRM.md)
  : DIF analysis dichotomous - requires having set up dif.variables

- [`RIdifThreshFigLR()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifThreshFigLR.md)
  : DIF PCM analysis with panel figure output for item thresholds

- [`RIdifThreshTblLR()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifThreshTblLR.md)
  : DIF PCM analysis with table output for item thresholds

- [`RIdifTileplot()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIdifTileplot.md)
  : Check response distribution prior to DIF analysis

- [`RIestThetas()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIestThetas.md)
  : Person location estimation

- [`RIestThetasCATr()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIestThetasCATr.md)
  : Person location estimation with parallel processing

- [`RIestThetasOLD()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIestThetasOLD.md)
  :

  Person location estimation (`catR` version)

- [`RIgetResidCor()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIgetResidCor.md)
  : Get simulation based cutoff values for Yen's Q^3^ residual
  correlations

- [`RIgetResidCorG2()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIgetResidCorG2.md)
  : Get simulation based cutoff values for G^2^ local dependency test

- [`RIgetfit()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIgetfit.md)
  : Get simulation based cutoff values for item fit values

- [`RIgetfitPlot()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIgetfitPlot.md)
  : Creates a plot with distribution of simulation based item fit values

- [`RIheatmap()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIheatmap.md)
  : Create a Guttman-like tileplot/heatmap

- [`RIinfitKfold()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIinfitKfold.md)
  : Cross-validation of conditional item infit

- [`RIinfitKfoldPlot()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIinfitKfoldPlot.md)
  : Plot k-fold infit based on raw output

- [`RIinfitLoc()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIinfitLoc.md)
  : Plot with infit ZSTD and item location ZSTD is sample size
  sensitive, see "RIitemfitPCM" for options

- [`RIitemCats()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIitemCats.md)
  : Individual item category probability plots

- [`RIitemHierarchy()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIitemHierarchy.md)
  : Create a figure showing items and thresholds (with CI)

- [`RIitemcols()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIitemcols.md)
  : Item response distribution figure

- [`RIitemfit()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIitemfit.md)
  : Calculate conditional infit MSQ statistics

- [`RIitemfitPCM()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIitemfitPCM.md)
  **\[deprecated\]** : Create table with Rasch PCM model item fit values
  for each item.

- [`RIitemfitPCM2()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIitemfitPCM2.md)
  **\[deprecated\]** : Create table with Rasch PCM model item fit values
  for each item.

- [`RIitemfitRM()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIitemfitRM.md)
  **\[deprecated\]** : Create table with Rasch dichotomous model item
  fit values for each item.

- [`RIitemparams()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIitemparams.md)
  : Item parameters summary

- [`RIlistItemsMargin()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIlistItemsMargin.md)
  : Create a table with the items used in a dataframe

- [`RIlistitems()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIlistitems.md)
  : Show items based on itemlabels file

- [`RIloadLoc()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIloadLoc.md)
  : Generates a plot showing the first residual contrast loadings based
  on a PCA of Rasch model residuals vs item locations.

- [`RImissing()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RImissing.md)
  : Creates a figure with item missing data descriptives for items

- [`RImissingP()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RImissingP.md)
  : Creates a figure with item missing data descriptives for
  participants

- [`RIoutfitLoc()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIoutfitLoc.md)
  : Plot with outfit ZSTD and item location ZSTD is sample size
  sensitive, see "RIitemfitPCM"

- [`RIpartgamDIF()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIpartgamDIF.md)
  : Partial gamma analysis of Differential Item Functioning

- [`RIpartgamLD()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIpartgamLD.md)
  : Partial gamma analysis of local dependence

- [`RIpboot()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIpboot.md) :
  Parametric bootstrapping function that outputs simulated datasets

- [`RIpcmPCA()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIpcmPCA.md)
  :

  Fits a Rasch PCM using eRm, and conducts a PCA of residuals to get
  eigenvalues using
  [`psych::pca()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/psych/man/principal.html) and
  reports the top 5 values.

- [`RIpfit()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIpfit.md) :
  Person fit

- [`RIrawdist()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIrawdist.md)
  : Floor/ceiling effects based on raw data (ordinal scores). Needs at
  least one data point in each response category to produce correct
  footnote text.

- [`RIreliability()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIreliability.md)
  : Reliability metrics

- [`RIresidcorr()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIresidcorr.md)
  : Correlation matrix of Rasch residuals using Yen's Q^3^

- [`RIresidcorrG2()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIresidcorrG2.md)
  : Correlation matrix of Rasch residuals using G^2^

- [`RIrestscore()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIrestscore.md)
  : Item-restscore

- [`RIrestscoreKfold()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIrestscoreKfold.md)
  : Cross-validation of item-restscore

- [`RIrmPCA()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIrmPCA.md) :

  Fits the Rasch model for dichotomous data using
  [`eRm::RM()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/eRm/man/RM.html), and conducts a PCA
  of residuals to get eigenvalues.

- [`RIscoreSE()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIscoreSE.md)
  : Raw sum score to logit score transformation table & figure

- [`RItargeting()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RItargeting.md)
  : Targeting, Wright map derivative.

- [`RItif()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RItif.md)
  **\[deprecated\]** : Reliability of test

- [`RItileplot()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RItileplot.md)
  : Create tile plot for all items, also showing the count of responses
  in each response category for each item

- [`RIu3poly()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RIu3poly.md)
  : Person fit with U3 for polytomous data

- [`RMUreliability()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RMUreliability.md)
  : Estimate reliability (Relative Measurement Uncertainty) from
  Bayesian measurement models

- [`RIrelRep()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/RelRep.md) :
  Reliability Representativeness of Coefficient Alpha or Omega

- [`SimPartialScore()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/SimPartialScore.md)
  : Simulate polytomous Rasch model data for a set of items

- [`SimPolyItem()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/SimPolyItem.md)
  : Simulate polytomous Rasch model data for a single item

- [`kbl_rise()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/kbl_rise.md)
  : A kableExtra function to simplify table creation

- [`theme_rise()`](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/reference/theme_rise.md)
  : A simple ggplot theme for RISE formatting

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [easyRasch package
  vignette](https://pgmj.github.io/easyRasch/articles/easyrasch-vignette.md):
